title: Elytranthe sumbawensis
intro: Elytranthe sumbawensis, also known as the Sumbawa vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential in treating conditions such as arthritis and cancer. The bioactive constituents of E. sumbawensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, E. sumbawensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are valued for their potential in treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Elytranthe sumbawensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Elytranthe, which is a small group of plants endemic to the island of Sumbawa in Indonesia. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Within the clade Asterids, Elytranthe sumbawensis is part of the larger group known as the Lamiales. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a distinct species within the Apocynaceae family.
